input.command:
git add architecture.md CLAUDE.md cloudRisk mcp-scanner && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Update submodule refs and add architecture docs

- cloudRisk: VPC peering scanner now persists vertices via saveToNeptune
- mcp-scanner: get_lambda_logs supports up to 24 hours
- architecture.md: documents scan pipeline, Neptune patterns, IAM roles

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
input.description: Commit submodule refs and docs in root repo

interceptorLog.reason: Judge: consistent (The agent is performing git status followed by staging and committing files, which aligns with the original task to commit and push all changes.)

input.prompt:
Explore the scanner domain in the cloudRisk module. I need a comprehensive view of the codebase structure and code. 

1. List all files under cloudRisk/src/domains/scanner/ recursively
2. Read the base class AWSDataGatherer and any shared scanner utilities
3. Read RULES.md in cloudRisk/ for coding conventions
4. Read at least 15-20 individual scanner files to understand patterns and spot inconsistencies

Focus on finding:
- Bugs (logic errors, missing error handling at boundaries, incorrect API calls)
- Pattern violations (not using credentials2, using accountId instead of accountID, missing rate limiting, incorrect Neptune operations)
- Inconsistencies between scanners (some doing things differently than others without reason)

Be very thorough - read as many scanner files as possible. Report back with file paths, line numbers, and specific code snippets for any issues found.
